description: Thorium Reader is a free and open-source EPUB reading application designed with sustainability as a core consideration. This report assesses conformance to Web Sustainability Guidelines principles.
evaluationMethodology: This report documents conformance evaluation of Thorium Reader Desktop version 3.0.0 against Web Sustainability Guidelines. The evaluation methodology included: review of user experience design sustainability, assessment of code and development practices, evaluation of infrastructure and deployment, and business strategy sustainability considerations.
conformanceSummary: Thorium Reader Desktop demonstrates strong commitment to web sustainability principles across user experience, development, and business practices. The application is lightweight, performant, open-source, and designed with accessibility and user respect as core principles.

strengths: Open-source approach supporting sustainability and transparency
Minimalist user interface with essential features only
Keyboard navigation and assistive technology support
Efficient resource usage with Electron framework
User preference customization without feature bloat
No tracking or unnecessary data collection
limitations: Electron framework heavier than pure web application
Some CSS and rendering features may be power-intensive
Mobile applications separate rather than unified platform

testingEnvironments: Windows 10+ with performance and resource monitoring
Testing with typical EPUB publications from various sources

changelog.description: Initial conformance report for Thorium Reader Desktop 3.0.0 against Web Sustainability Guidelines

wsg.name: WSG
wsg.title: Web Sustainability Guidelines

productInfo.name: Thorium Reader Desktop
productInfo.description: Thorium Reader Desktop is a free, open-source EPUB reading application available for Windows, macOS, and Linux. The application is free, with no ads and no private data leaks.
productInfo.documentation: English, French, Spanish, and multiple other languages

wsg.name: WSG
wsg.title: Web Sustainability Guidelines
Examine and disclose any external factors interacting with your project
wsg.description: Identify, track, and publicly disclose negative external factors.

wsg.title: Understand user requirements or constraints, resolving barriers to access
wsg.notes: Thorium Reader provides extensive accessibility features and user customization options
wsg.description: Understand the audience and their requirements, remove their constraints and barriers, and give them an equal role in decision making.

wsg.title: Integrate sustainability into every stage of the ideation process
wsg.description: Optimize materials created at every stage of the process and consider the needs of and impact on the planet, and other affected parties.

wsg.title: Minimize non-essential content, interactivity, or journeys
wsg.notes: Interface includes only essential features and controls
wsg.description: Use distraction and clutter-free design, showing the user only what they need without interruptions or wasted resource consumption.

wsg.title: Ensure that navigation and wayfinding are well-structured
wsg.description: Provide good information architecture that signposts users, both human and machine, to what they need, so they can reach their destination as efficiently as possible.

wsg.title: Design to assist and not to distract
wsg.description: Provide engagement that keeps the user's focus where it needs to be, avoiding anything that will either reduce efficiency or artificially prolong engagement.

wsg.title: Avoid being manipulative or deceptive
wsg.description: Avoid using patterns, content, tools, or techniques that may artificially manipulate or deceive the user and waste energy.

wsg.title: Make deliverables understandable and reusable
wsg.description: Create discoverable, well documented deliverables so that future users can find and make use of them without requiring assistance or expending unnecessary time and resources.

wsg.title: Use a design system for interface consistency
wsg.description: Keep the components of the project as consistent and clear as possible by using a design system to organize reusable features.

wsg.title: Provide clear, inclusive content with purpose
wsg.notes: Clear, accessible interface with multilingual support
wsg.description: Provide content that meets the needs of the audience, ensuring it is formatted for readability and incorporating SEO for visibility, so they can reach their destination as efficiently as possible.

wsg.title: Optimize media for sustainability
wsg.notes: Efficient media rendering without unnecessary optimizations
wsg.description: Ensure all media - including audio, video, animation, images, and other novel formats, when deemed necessary in the design, are optimized, correctly sized and formatted, use lazy loading where appropriate, and managed effectively.

wsg.title: Ensure animation is proportionate and easy to control
wsg.description: Ensure any animation deemed necessary is not overdone and that the user can control its ability to run.

wsg.title: Use optimized and appropriate web typography
wsg.description: Provide custom fonts in the most suitable file format, but with a preference for pre-installed typefaces where possible.

wsg.title: Offer suitable alternatives for every format used
wsg.description: Integrate fallbacks so that if something cannot load or is not compatible, there is an accessible alternative to ensure equal access to the content.

wsg.title: Provide accessible, usable, minimal web forms
wsg.description: Ensure forms are as minimalistic and friction-free as possible, reduce the impact of submitting, and only collecting required data.

wsg.title: Provide useful notifications
wsg.description: Ensure any required notifications or alerts are clearly explained before activation, and that the user can both control and change them.

wsg.title: Reduce the impact of downloadable and physical documents
wsg.description: Reduce the need for physical documents as much as possible by allowing the saving of well-labeled, optimized digital downloads and having a print style sheet.

wsg.title: Involve users and contributors early in the project
wsg.description: Document the test process for new features with real users and validate the results, ensuring resources and training are provided to maintain the project's viability and minimize rework.

wsg.title: Audit and test for bugs or issues requiring resolution
wsg.description: Regularly audit for issues or problems, running tests at regular intervals in both simulated and real-world scenarios to ensure stability of the project.

wsg.title: Verify that real-world users can successfully use your work
wsg.description: Factor the ways people use the project into your decision making and evaluate how this can be optimized for both people and the planet.

wsg.title: Regularly test and maintain compatibility
wsg.notes: Maintained across Windows 10+, macOS 10.13+, and multiple Linux distributions
wsg.description: Produce a regularly updated compatibility policy that details support levels, scenarios tested against, and technology used to benefit users.

wsg.title: Set goals based on performance and energy impact
wsg.description: Account for the difference in energy intensity that different types of content may have on a project and set goals based on what you would like to achieve.

wsg.title: Remove unnecessary or redundant information
wsg.notes: Code is optimized for size and performance
wsg.description: Remove redundant characters from code to reduce the amount of data being stored and transferred to devices.

wsg.title: Modularize bandwidth-heavy components
wsg.description: Break down large components into smaller, self-contained pieces that can be requested only when required to save bandwidth.

wsg.title: Remove unnecessary code
wsg.description: Clear out dead or unused code as it builds up in a project to reduce the amount of wasted data being transferred.

wsg.title: Avoid redundancy and duplication in code
wsg.description: Improve solutions rather than recreating them, using good organization strategies to simplify code where possible and reduce redundancy.

wsg.title: Give third parties the same priority as first parties during assessment
wsg.description: Avoid using large third-party libraries and frameworks where possible, and where these must be used, self host, defer loading, allow the user to object, and provide alternatives.

wsg.title: Ensure code follows good semantic practices
wsg.notes: Open-source codebase follows semantic best practices
wsg.description: Use semantic markup free of optional or non-standard code, use the latest version of technical specifications, and only using custom solutions when native ones are insufficient.

wsg.title: Defer the loading of non-critical resources
wsg.description: Avoid render-blocking slowdowns by asynchronously loading, deferring, or prioritizing external resources as appropriate.

wsg.title: Provide information to help understand the usefulness of a page
wsg.description: Ensure that accessibility tooling can access a project to reduce unnecessary journeys.

wsg.title: Validate form errors and account for tooling requirements
wsg.description: Label forms correctly while also checking for errors both prior to and during submission to reduce the impact of erroneous data entry.

wsg.title: Structure metadata for machine readability
wsg.description: Include the required metadata and structured microdata to increase the visibility and findability of resources.

wsg.title: Use sustainability beneficial user preference media queries
wsg.description: Use sustainable media queries, such as dark mode and scripting media queries, to customize an interface to suit user preferences and reduce emissions.

wsg.title: Ensure layouts work for different devices and requirements
wsg.description: Use a mix of carbon-aware design techniques to adapt to different layouts, different modes of interaction, and also progressively enhance content.

wsg.title: Use standards-based JavaScript and APIs
wsg.description: Use JavaScript in an energy-efficient and accessible way, only using APIs and making external calls when this enhances a project's sustainability.

wsg.title: Ensure that your code is secure
wsg.description: Regularly check code for exploitable security issues that could affect both users and hosting infrastructure.

wsg.title: Use dependencies appropriately and ensure maintenance
wsg.notes: Dependencies are carefully selected and regularly maintained
wsg.description: Use libraries and frameworks only where necessary, when plain code or smaller packages are inappropriate for the use case, and they are kept up to date.

wsg.title: Include expected and beneficial files
wsg.description: Always provide expected files and optionally offer environmentally or otherwise beneficial files to enhance the project.

wsg.title: Use the most efficient solution for your service
wsg.description: Identify the project requirements, use the most efficient approach, aim for static over dynamic where possible, and consider the impact of extensions and components.

wsg.title: Use the latest stable language version
wsg.description: Consider the environmental impact of the chosen syntax language, while always ensuring you use the latest build of any language or framework.

wsg.title: Reduce the number and complexity of database queries
wsg.description: Optimize any information provided via databases, both in terms of access schedules and how queries are sent and received.

wsg.title: Use sustainable hosting
wsg.notes: Open-source software available for self-hosting or deployed on sustainable infrastructure
wsg.description: Ensure that hosting and domain infrastructure and service providers offer monitoring of resource use capabilities, low-carbon operations, and proper equipment maintenance and disposal.

wsg.title: Optimize caching and support offline access
wsg.notes: Application supports offline reading with local caching
wsg.description: Use caching on all appropriate resources, prioritizing the ability to use the resource offline if possible.

wsg.title: Reduce data transfer with compression
wsg.description: Use compression on all appropriate resources either before or as part of the transfer process.

wsg.title: Setup necessary error pages and redirection links
wsg.description: Use error and redirection handling on projects to reduce the impact of unnecessary journeys or unhelpful detours.

wsg.title: Avoid maintaining unnecessary virtualized environments or containers
wsg.description: Reduce the number of unnecessary virtualized environments to save hardware utilization.

wsg.title: Use automation wisely
wsg.description: Automate recurring tasks only where this saves resources, such as scaling services to reduce consumption or handling suspicious activity.

wsg.title: Define the frequency of data refreshes
wsg.description: Decide when to refresh data based on a balance of user and sustainability considerations.

wsg.title: Back up critical data at routine intervals
wsg.description: Back up data at regular intervals to ensure that there are failsafes that can be relied upon should an issue occur.

wsg.title: Consider the impact and requirements of data processing
wsg.description: Account for the energy involved in data transfer, considering factors such as the protocol used, whether it is processed client- or server-side, and the environment used.

wsg.title: Use Content Delivery Networks (CDNs) appropriately
wsg.description: Use CDNs where they bring a sustainability benefit, ensuring the provider itself is sustainable, has nodes local to an audience, and they are only used to serve static resources on them.

wsg.title: Ensure infrastructure fits project requirements
wsg.description: Maintain the infrastructure you require rather than over-provisioning. Autoscale to meet demand and preserve hardware resources.

wsg.title: Store data according to the needs of your users
wsg.description: Set expiration dates on data so it can be archived, made available offline, or removed when no longer used or required, with the remaining content tagged for future management.

wsg.title: Have an ethical and sustainable product strategy
wsg.description: Produce policies and documents showcasing evidence, achievements, and onboarding underpinned by good governance.

wsg.title: Assign a sustainability advocate
wsg.description: Appoint a sustainability advocate who is provided with what they require to speak about and for issues relating to sustainability.

wsg.title: Inform, raise awareness, and train for sustainability
wsg.description: Drive sustainability awareness both externally and internally with active encouragement and routine training offered where necessary.

wsg.title: Communicate the environmental impact of user choices
wsg.description: Empower users, allowing them to make decisions when their choices can influence the environmental impact they have.

wsg.title: Calculate the environmental impact
wsg.description: Calculate the environmental impact of the project and any tooling you use, and even that of a competitor, where possible, as this may be useful for comparison.

wsg.title: Define clear organizational sustainability goals and metrics
wsg.description: Publish a set of sustainability goals that can be used to track progress over a period of time.

wsg.title: Validate efforts using established third-party certifications
wsg.description: Obtain and maintain third-party certifications to externally validate progress toward sustainability goals.

wsg.title: Support mandatory disclosures and reporting
wsg.description: Create policies and documents showing evidence of commitment towards impact progress, how this is achieved, and any applicable regulations you are meeting.

wsg.title: Create one or more impact business models
wsg.description: Produce a document that shows how the organisation is taking steps to become as resilient as possible.

wsg.title: Follow a product management and maintenance strategy
wsg.notes: Active development and maintenance with regular releases
wsg.description: Explain through documentation how projects will be maintained, managed, resourced, and note issues while tracking measurements over time.

wsg.title: Implement continuous improvement procedures
wsg.description: Continuously improve projects through regular reviews and iteration, integrating technical debt and security management, determining if new or existing functionality is required.

wsg.title: Document future updates and evolutions
wsg.description: Provide documentation to help users find their way whenever functionality changes.

wsg.title: Establish if a digital product or service is necessary
wsg.notes: Thorium Reader addresses genuine user need for accessible EPUB reading
wsg.description: Determine whether a project is necessary, eliminating obstacles for users, and aligning with SDGs and sustainability principles.

wsg.title: Provide a supplier standards of practice document
wsg.description: Vet suppliers before onboarding, then collaborate to promote and encourage better sustainability practices.

wsg.title: Share economic benefits
wsg.description: Share benefits associated with being sustainably resilient with workers, incentivizing efforts, and advocating for those involved to meet sustainability goals.

wsg.title: Share decision-making power with affected parties
wsg.description: Ensure that all affected parties have the opportunity to get involved in helping the organisation become more sustainable.

wsg.title: Use Justice, Equity, Diversity, Inclusion (JEDI) practices
wsg.notes: Multilingual support, accessibility focus, and inclusive design
wsg.description: Implement JEDI practices to foster inclusive hiring practices within an organization, showing evidence of improvement over time.

wsg.title: Promote responsible data practices
wsg.description: Provide publicly accessible versions of documents required by law in a sustainable, easy-to-consume format, while also observing ethical data retention practices.

wsg.title: Implement appropriate data management procedures
wsg.description: Allow users to manage, control, and delete their data upon request, while making outdated content searchable via a lightweight and simple archive.

wsg.title: Establish responsible practices around AI and emerging or disruptive technologies
wsg.description: Implement policies, training, and auditing practices for AI and emerging or disruptive to ensure sustainability upon implementation, while accounting for associated legal issues.

wsg.title: Include responsible financial policies
wsg.description: Ensure that a project is suitably budgeted for and funded through sustainable and ethical sources.

wsg.title: Include organizational philanthropy policies
wsg.description: Provide the opportunity to give back to the community through charitable or volunteer efforts via your organisation.

wsg.title: Plan for a digital product or service's care and end-of-life
wsg.description: Produce documentation to ensure that when a project is shut down, users are made aware of what will happen to their data.

wsg.title: Include e-waste, right to repair, and recycling policies
wsg.description: Include policies and documentation explaining how sustainable e-waste, recycling, repairing, and refurbishment are handled.

wsg.title: Define performance and environmental budgets
wsg.description: Set budgets, taking people, planet, and performance into account alongside baselines, targets, evidence, and resources to help meet KPIs.

wsg.title: Use open source where possible
wsg.notes: Entirely open-source project under BSD-3-Clause license
wsg.description: Produce a policy around open collaboration and contribution to creating and sustaining open source projects.

wsg.title: Create a business continuity and disaster recovery plan
wsg.description: Create a plan of action with a system to recover from failure or alert users if or when a loss of service occurs.
